founder.name: Swayam Shah
founder.title: Founder using Tigerclaw to find the warmest investor path

connector.name: Harrison Dahme
connector.title: CTO @ Hack VC | Engineering & Ventures | Agent Framework & Blockchain Lab Co-Founder

likelyInvestorDestination.name: Hack VC venture path
likelyInvestorDestination.description: A warm AI, infrastructure, and crypto-adjacent investor route that stays specific instead of broad.

warmPathSummary: Tigerclaw identified Harrison as the strongest current investor-facing route because the relationship is already warm, the venture context is direct, and the first ask can be framed as a focused calibration conversation instead of an immediate fundraising request.
whyThisPerson: Harrison is compelling because the path is already venture-adjacent and the relationship carries real founder memory: recent AI-agent and infrastructure discussion, shared operator language, and enough trust to reopen the thread naturally.
whyNow: The timing works because the founder can follow up with a short Hack VC fit memo and a traction snapshot while the conversation context is still recent, which makes the next step feel like a continuation rather than a cold ask.
whyThisRankedAboveOthers: It ranked above softer bridge candidates because the route ends closer to actual capital allocation, yet still preserves warmth. It ranked above a direct Justin-first route because Harrison allows a narrower, lower-friction re-entry into the venture conversation.
suggestedAsk: Message Harrison with a short context refresh, attach a concise Hack VC fit memo plus a Tigerclaw traction snapshot, and ask for a brief calibration call before requesting any broader investor path.
supportingProof: Recent venture conversation around AI agents, infrastructure, and crypto-adjacent product strategy.
A short Tigerclaw traction snapshot and investor-fit memo tailored to Hack VC.
Shared operator context that makes the message feel like a continuation of an existing conversation instead of a generic founder pitch.
likelyObjection: If the outreach jumps too fast to a broad fundraise intro, the warmth of the relationship can collapse into a transactional ask.
nextBestStep: Send a context-first reply, keep the ask to a short calibration conversation, and only widen it into investor routing after the fit is confirmed.
draftMessage: Hey Harrison, great chatting recently about AI-agent infrastructure and crypto-adjacent product strategy. I am building Tigerclaw, which helps founders turn their existing network into the warmest investor path, and I think there is a specific overlap with the kind of founder and infrastructure context Hack VC sees. I put together a very short note on why I think the fit is real, plus a concise traction snapshot. Would you be open to a quick calibration call, and if it resonates, point me toward the right next investor path rather than me guessing from the outside?
